untranslatable_reason: ABW (Ascending Broadening Wedge) is a complex candlestick pattern requiring visual identification of diverging trendlines with specific height/width combinations over 61-99 days. The pattern detection logic (trendline divergence, formation length measurement, and height/width classification) cannot be expressed using only the allowed indicators and operators. The signal requires pattern recognition capabilities beyond price-based technical indicators.
translation_notes: Attempted approximate translation using Donchian breakouts as a proxy for wedge pattern breakouts (both directions supported). However, this does not capture the core ABW pattern characteristics: (1) converging/diverging trendlines, (2) specific formation length 61-99 days, (3) height and width classifications showing poor performance for short/wide combinations, (4) volume trend showing little influence. A proper implementation would require a dedicated ABW pattern detector that analyzes trendline slopes and price range expansion metrics not available in the standard indicator set.
